history.historicalImportance: Bhagalpur is one of the oldest cities of Bihar and was the capital of the ancient Anga Mahajanapada. It is internationally famous for Bhagalpuri Silk and Vikramshila Mahavihara.
history.formationHistory: Bhagalpur district was constituted during the British administration in the 18th century and has remained an important administrative and commercial centre of eastern Bihar.
history.freedomMovement: The people of Bhagalpur actively participated in the Indian freedom movement, including the Non-Cooperation Movement, Civil Disobedience Movement and Quit India Movement.
history.archaeologicalImportance: The district is home to the world-famous Vikramshila Mahavihara, an ancient Buddhist university, along with several historical and archaeological sites.
historicalTimeline: Ancient period - Capital of the Anga Mahajanapada
6th–12th century - Vikramshila Mahavihara flourished under the Pala dynasty
Medieval period - Ruled by the Delhi Sultanate and Mughal Empire
1765 - Came under British administration after the Treaty of Allahabad
Present - Major silk, education and commercial centre of Bihar

geography.location: Bhagalpur district is located in the eastern part of Bihar on the southern bank of the Ganga River.
geography.climate: Subtropical climate with hot summers, a monsoon season and mild winters.
geography.forests: Limited forest cover, mainly in the southern parts adjoining Banka district.

agriculture.dairyDevelopment: Dairy farming is an important source of income for rural households, supported by cooperative milk collection.
agriculture.fisheries: Fish farming is widely practiced in ponds, oxbow lakes and along the Ganga River.

infrastructure.roadNetwork: Bhagalpur has a well-developed road network connected through National Highways, State Highways and district roads.
infrastructure.airConnectivity: The nearest airport is Lok Nayak Jayaprakash Airport, Patna. Deoghar Airport also provides regional connectivity.
infrastructure.publicTransport: Bihar State Road Transport Corporation (BSRTC), private buses, auto-rickshaws, e-rickshaws and taxis provide public transport.
infrastructure.electricity: Most urban and rural areas have electricity connectivity under the Bihar power distribution network.
infrastructure.waterSupply: Urban areas receive piped water supply, while rural areas depend on hand pumps, tube wells and government water supply schemes.
infrastructure.internetConnectivity: 4G and 5G mobile networks are available in most parts of the district, along with broadband and fiber internet services.

education.governmentSchools: More than 2,000 government primary, middle and secondary schools operate across the district.
education.privateSchools: Several CBSE, ICSE and Bihar Board affiliated private schools are located in Bhagalpur.
medicalColleges: Jawaharlal Nehru Medical College and Hospital (JLNMCH), Bhagalpur
notableEducationalInstitutions: Jawaharlal Nehru Medical College and Hospital

healthcare.districtHospital: Jawaharlal Nehru Medical College and Hospital (JLNMCH), Bhagalpur
healthcare.primaryHealthCentres: Primary Health Centres (PHCs) are available across all blocks of the district.
medicalColleges: Jawaharlal Nehru Medical College and Hospital (JLNMCH), Bhagalpur
healthcare.ambulanceServices: 102 and 108 ambulance services are available throughout the district.
notableHospitals: Jawaharlal Nehru Medical College and Hospital (JLNMCH)

cultureAndHeritage.traditionalDress: Men traditionally wear Dhoti-Kurta and women wear Saree and Salwar-Kameez.
culturalOrganizations: District Art and Culture Office, Bhagalpur
Tilka Manjhi Bhagalpur University Cultural Centre
Department of Art, Culture and Youth, Bihar

sportsAcademies: Bihar State Sports Authority Training Centre
sportsAchievements: District athletes regularly participate in state-level competitions
School and college teams represent Bhagalpur in Bihar-level tournaments
Growing participation in athletics and cricket

bankingAndFinance.atms: ATMs of major public sector and private sector banks are available across the district.
bankingAndFinance.digitalBanking: UPI, Internet Banking, Mobile Banking and AEPS services are widely available.
financialInclusionPrograms: Pradhan Mantri Jeevan Jyoti Bima Yojana

governanceAndPublicServices.districtAdministration: Bhagalpur district is administered by the District Magistrate (DM) and the district administration under the Government of Bihar.
governanceAndPublicServices.policeAdministration: Law and order is maintained by Bihar Police under the Senior Superintendent of Police (SSP), Bhagalpur.
governanceAndPublicServices.fireServices: Fire stations operate in Bhagalpur city, Naugachhia, Kahalgaon and other major areas of the district.
governanceAndPublicServices.publicDistributionSystem: Essential commodities are distributed through the Public Distribution System (PDS) under the Bihar Government.
eGovernanceServices: Bihar Right to Public Services Portal

lawAndOrder.trafficPolice: Traffic Police regulate traffic and road safety in Bhagalpur city and major urban areas.
lawAndOrder.disasterManagement: The District Disaster Management Authority (DDMA), Bhagalpur coordinates flood management, disaster preparedness and emergency response.

environment.forestCover: Moderate forest cover, mainly in the southern parts of the district and along the Ganga river ecosystem.

mediaAndCommunication.postalServices: India Post provides postal, Speed Post, parcel, banking and other postal services across the district.
mediaAndCommunication.telecommunication: 4G and 5G mobile connectivity is available in most urban areas, while 4G coverage is available across the district.

stateRankings: One of the major commercial and educational districts of Bihar
Known as the Silk City of Bihar
Important centre for silk production and higher education
nationalRankings: Bhagalpuri Silk is nationally and internationally renowned
Vikramshila Mahavihara is one of India's important ancient Buddhist universities
Home to the Vikramshila Gangetic Dolphin Sanctuary
notableAchievements: Vikramshila Mahavihara is a globally recognized Buddhist heritage site
Conservation of the endangered Gangetic Dolphin
Major educational hub of eastern Bihar
developmentIndicators: Expansion of road and rail connectivity
Development of tourism and riverfront infrastructure
Expansion of industrial and commercial activities
records: Bhagalpur is popularly known as the Silk City of India
Home to the historic Vikramshila Mahavihara
Contains the Vikramshila Gangetic Dolphin Sanctuary

importantDates: 18 October 1875 - Bhagalpur district was reorganized during the British period
15 August 1947 - Became part of independent India
additionalInformation: Bhagalpur is internationally famous for Bhagalpuri Silk.
Vikramshila Mahavihara was one of the greatest ancient Buddhist universities of India.
The Vikramshila Gangetic Dolphin Sanctuary protects the endangered Gangetic Dolphin.
Bhagalpur is one of the major educational, commercial and cultural centres of Bihar.
